metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete proving command for the `.agents/rules/rebar.md` criterion (e.g., `diff <(grep -v '^#' .agents/rules/rebar.md) <(grep -v '^#' AGENTS.md)` or a `rg` command that confirms a canonical pointer line exists) and replace 'consistent / no drift' with a specific, observable outcome such as 'file contains only a pointer line referencing AGENTS.md' or 'file content matches the designated section of AGENTS.md'.
evidence: `.agents/rules/rebar.md` is consistent with `AGENTS.md` (pointer or aligned), no drift.
No `rg`, `diff`, or other concrete command is provided to prove consistency or absence of drift.
findings.finding: The `.agents/rules/rebar.md` acceptance criterion has no proving command and uses subjective language ('consistent with AGENTS.md', 'no drift') with no measurable threshold — an agent cannot deterministically verify it is done.
scenarios: An executor who rewrites rebar.md differently from AGENTS.md has no automated signal that they've drifted.
A reviewer cannot confirm the criterion is met without a manual side-by-side comparison that is not specified.
findings.suggested_fix: If the chosen approach is pointer-only: `rg -c 'See AGENTS\.md' .agents/rules/rebar.md` as the proving command, with the AC reworded to 'contains only a forward-pointer to AGENTS.md with no duplicated content'. If aligned content is allowed: specify that `diff .agents/rules/rebar.md <(sed -n '/^## Rebar/,/^## /p' AGENTS.md)` exits 0.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State a default decision for the optional per-tool pointer files in the plan (e.g., 'default: skip unless a concrete tool integration already in use in this repo is identified during the CLAUDE.md sweep; if added, must be a single-line pointer only') so the executor can act without stopping.
evidence: 'Decide and record whether to add optional per-tool pointers' — 'decide' is left open.
'Decision on optional Copilot/Cursor/Gemini per-tool files is recorded; any added are pointers, not duplicated content.' — records only the constraint, not the default outcome.
findings.finding: AC item (4) defers the 'decide and record' choice on optional per-tool pointer files (Copilot/Cursor/Gemini) to the executor with no stated default, making it a placeholder that requires the executor to make an unguided design decision mid-task.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — 'Decision on optional Copilot/Cursor/Gemini per-tool files' bullet; What section item (4)
scenarios: An executor who is uncertain about Copilot in-editor configuration may stop and ask, violating the no-clarification-needed requirement.
Two executors working the same plan could legitimately reach opposite conclusions (add vs. skip) and both satisfy the AC.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a sentence such as: 'Default decision: do not add new per-tool pointer files in this story; record that decision as a comment in the PR. Add only if the CLAUDE.md sweep discovers an existing `.github/copilot-instructions.md` or equivalent already in the repo.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Rewrite the CLAUDE.md sweep AC as an outcome: specify either (a) a count assertion (`rg -n 'CLAUDE\.md' --glob '!CLAUDE.md' | wc -l` equals N where N is the known intentional count) or (b) an allowlist check that fails if unexpected matches appear, replacing 'reviewed line-by-line' with a deterministic pass/fail criterion.
evidence: '`rg -n 'CLAUDE\.md' --glob '!CLAUDE.md'` reviewed line-by-line in the change' — 'reviewed' is process, not outcome.
No command asserts that remaining matches are intentionally allowed (e.g., an allowlist count assertion).
findings.finding: The CLAUDE.md reference sweep AC uses process-framed language ('reviewed line-by-line') rather than a pass/fail outcome, so the criterion can be satisfied by performing the review without actually updating anything — the end-state (zero unintentional stale references remain) is not stated as the verifiable outcome.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — 'Every remaining `CLAUDE.md` reference in the repo is intentional or updated' bullet
scenarios: An executor who runs the command and eyeballs results without updating any file has technically 'reviewed line-by-line' and could mark the AC done.
A stale reference left in CONTRIBUTING.md after a rushed review silently passes because the AC asks only for review, not for zero unintentional references.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace the proving note with: `rg -n 'CLAUDE\.md' --glob '!CLAUDE.md' | grep -v -F -f .claude-md-allowlist` exits 1 (no unallowlisted matches). Create `.claude-md-allowlist` containing the intentionally retained references (e.g., the thin CLAUDE.md file itself, CHANGELOG entries). Alternatively: assert the count equals zero after the sweep with `rg -c 'CLAUDE\.md' --glob '!CLAUDE.md' --glob '!CHANGELOG.md' == 0`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Merge the two AC lists into one authoritative set that covers all four What-section deliverables plus the criteria_routing.json/plan_review_T5e.md updates, eliminating divergence between the original AC and the Revised AC block.
evidence: Original AC item 2: '.agents/rules/rebar.md is consistent with AGENTS.md (pointer or aligned), no drift.' — absent from Revised AC
Original AC item 4: 'Decision on optional Copilot/Cursor/Gemini per-tool files is recorded' — absent from Revised AC
Revised AC item 2: 'The plan-review criteria text (criteria_routing.json + plan_review_T5e.md)…are updated' — absent from original AC and from the What section
findings.finding: The plan contains two separate Acceptance Criteria lists (original AC and 'Revised Acceptance Criteria' in De-risking) that diverge: the original AC covers `.agents/rules/rebar.md` consistency and the Copilot/Cursor/Gemini pointer decision, while the Revised AC omits both; meanwhile the Revised AC adds `criteria_routing.json`/`plan_review_T5e.md` updates absent from the original. An implementer cannot determine which list is authoritative, and work covered only in one list may be dropped.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria section vs. 'Revised Acceptance Criteria' block inside De-risking results
scenarios: An implementer treats the Revised AC as superseding and skips .agents/rules/rebar.md reconciliation and the per-tool-pointer decision.
An implementer treats the original AC as authoritative and skips the criteria_routing.json/plan_review_T5e.md updates.
findings.suggested_fix: Remove the 'Revised Acceptance Criteria' sub-block and instead append the two new criteria (criteria_routing.json update; config.toml comment) to the original AC list, preserving the .agents/rules/rebar.md and per-tool-pointer criteria.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a fifth deliverable to the What section explicitly describing the criteria_routing.json + plan_review_T5e.md CLAUDE.md→AGENTS.md text update, so every AC item maps to a described work item.
evidence: What section lists exactly 4 numbered deliverables; none mentions criteria_routing.json or plan_review_T5e.md

De-risking section: 'Also update the plan-review CRITERIA text so the gate's own coaching points at the new file: src/rebar/llm/plan_review/criteria_routing.json:407 and src/rebar/llm/reviewers/plan_review_T5e.md:10'
Revised AC item 2 verifies this update — but there is no What-section description of what the update entails or why
findings.finding: The `criteria_routing.json` and `plan_review_T5e.md` updates are described in the De-risking section and verified in the Revised AC, but are absent from the What section's deliverable list — they are uncovered work with no corresponding description of the change required.
findings.location: What section (items 1–4) vs. De-risking results paragraph 'Also update the plan-review CRITERIA text'
scenarios: Implementer scopes work from the What section, misses the criteria_routing.json change, and the Revised AC fails.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to What: '(5) Update `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/criteria_routing.json` (line 407) and `src/rebar/llm/reviewers/plan_review_T5e.md` (line 10) to replace "CLAUDE.md" references with "AGENTS.md" so the plan-review gate's own coaching is consistent.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit rollback note (e.g., 'if AGENTS.md is absent, restore `project_doc_fallback_filenames=["CLAUDE.md"]`') or an expand-contract sequencing guard (gate this story's merge on AGENTS.md being present in the repo) to satisfy the T4 reversibility requirement.
evidence: Scope: 'Depends on: the AGENTS.md story and the CLAUDE.md story (sweep runs after the canonical files exist)' — dependency stated but no guard or rollback described

De-risking: 'Codex reads AGENTS.md natively as primary; update the temporary…until migrated comment…rather than relying on the fallback' — removal of fallback is prescribed without a revert instruction
No rollback/back-out step appears anywhere in the plan
findings.finding: The plan removes the CLAUDE.md fallback from `.codex/config.toml` and depends on the AGENTS.md story having landed first, but provides no explicit rollback or back-out mechanism if this story is executed while AGENTS.md does not yet exist (e.g., stories run out of order or the AGENTS.md story is reverted).
findings.location: Scope section ('Depends on: the AGENTS.md story') and What section item (1)
scenarios: AGENTS.md story is reverted or delayed; this story has already removed the fallback; Codex loses all project guidance with no documented recovery path.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a 'Rollback' note to the plan: 'If this story must be reverted before AGENTS.md is confirmed stable, restore the original project_doc_fallback_filenames=["CLAUDE.md"] line in .codex/config.toml. CI should block merge of this story if AGENTS.md does not exist at HEAD.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Merge the two AC blocks into one authoritative list (or explicitly mark the revised block as 'replaces the above'), ensuring `.agents/rules/rebar.md` consistency and the Copilot/Cursor/Gemini decision-record items are preserved.
evidence: Original AC item: '.agents/rules/rebar.md is consistent with AGENTS.md (pointer or aligned), no drift.'
Original AC item: 'Decision on optional Copilot/Cursor/Gemini per-tool files is recorded; any added are pointers, not duplicated content.'
Revised AC omits both items entirely, replacing them with three different bullets.
ABSENCE: no merge instruction or 'supersedes' note between the two AC blocks
findings.finding: The plan contains two distinct, partially-conflicting AC blocks ('## Acceptance Criteria' and the 'Revised Acceptance Criteria' appended under 'De-risking results'). The revised block silently drops two items present in the original: (1) `.agents/rules/rebar.md` consistency verification and (2) the recorded decision on optional Copilot/Cursor/Gemini pointer files. An implementer who reads only the revised block — the more specific, command-bearing one — would omit those checks, leaving potential drift in `rebar.md` unverified and the per-tool file decision unrecorded.
findings.location: ## Acceptance Criteria (original block) vs. 'Revised Acceptance Criteria' under De-risking results
scenarios: Implementer reads the more specific revised AC (which has the rg commands) and treats it as authoritative, skips rebar.md alignment check.
Reviewer checks only revised AC during PR review, misses the per-tool file decision record requirement.
findings.suggested_fix: Delete the original '## Acceptance Criteria' block and promote the revised block (under de-risking) to be the sole AC section, adding back the two dropped items: (1) '`.agents/rules/rebar.md` is consistent with `AGENTS.md` (pointer or aligned), verified by manual review' and (2) 'Decision on optional per-tool pointer files (Copilot, Cursor, Gemini) is recorded in this story or an ADR; any added files are pointers only.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Consolidate the two Acceptance Criteria sections into one authoritative list that includes all goals: .codex/config.toml update, .agents/rules/rebar.md alignment, repo-wide CLAUDE.md sweep, optional per-tool pointer decision record, plan-review criteria text update, and CI green.
evidence: Original AC includes: '.agents/rules/rebar.md is consistent with AGENTS.md (pointer or aligned), no drift.' and 'Decision on optional Copilot/Cursor/Gemini per-tool files is recorded; any added are pointers, not duplicated content.'
Revised AC omits both of these, listing only three bullets covering rg sweep, plan-review criteria text, and CI.
No statement such as 'the revised AC supersedes the original' or 'the revised AC is additive' exists.
findings.finding: The plan contains two separate Acceptance Criteria sections (original AC block and 'Revised Acceptance Criteria' in the de-risking section) and does not state which is authoritative. The revised list silently drops two goals from the original: (a) `.agents/rules/rebar.md` alignment proof and (b) decision-recording for optional per-tool pointer files. An implementer following only the revised AC would miss those goals, undermining E3's 'each non-deferred goal has a faithful, end-state-observable proof' requirement.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria section vs. 'Revised Acceptance Criteria' in De-risking results
scenarios: Agent implements only the revised AC, skips rebar.md reconciliation and the per-tool-pointer decision record, and the PR passes CI but leaves drift and an unrecorded decision.
findings.suggested_fix: Delete the 'Revised Acceptance Criteria' heading and merge its three bullets into the original AC block (they are additive, not replacements), so the single AC list covers all six goals with their rg-proof commands.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm or drop the Gemini `context.fileName` note: either name the specific Gemini tool/product that reads this field (with a vendor citation) and add a proving step to the AC, or remove it from scope to avoid introducing unverified config surface.
evidence: Search for `context.fileName` returns zero hits in the entire repo.
No Gemini context-injection config file (e.g., `context.json`, `.gemini/context`) exists anywhere in the repo.
The plan offers no vendor citation for what Gemini tool reads a `context.fileName` field or file.
Contrast: `.github/copilot-instructions.md` is a documented GitHub Copilot feature; `.codex/config.toml` has `project_doc_fallback_filenames` which is a concrete, repo-present key.
findings.finding: The plan proposes adding a Gemini `context.fileName` note as an optional per-tool pointer, but no such Gemini tool convention exists anywhere in the codebase (zero search hits for `context.fileName`), and it is not a documented/standardized Gemini context-injection mechanism visible from the repo. Adding it without a concrete vendor reference or existing consumer would introduce dead config surface (a new file or config key with no verified reader).
scenarios: An implementer adds a `context.json` or similar file pointing to `AGENTS.md` for a Gemini tool that does not actually read it, leaving dead config surface with no consumer.
A future reviewer cannot determine which Gemini tool this targets or how to verify it is active.
findings.suggested_fix: Add an AC line: 'If the Gemini pointer is added, cite the specific Gemini tool + docs URL that reads the nominated file, and verify with `rg` that the file is present and non-empty after the change.' Otherwise, scope the decision to Copilot only and note Gemini as a future story.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify whether `.codex/config.toml` is a repo-tracked file (create it if so) or a user-level install documented in `scripts/session-shims/codex-config.toml`; reconcile the AC prove command accordingly.
evidence: No search hits for `.codex/config.toml` as a repo-tracked file
Only Codex-related repo file found: `scripts/session-shims/codex-config.toml` containing only `[shell_environment_policy]` — no `project_doc_fallback_filenames` key
Plan quote: '.codex/config.toml's project_doc_fallback_filenames=["CLAUDE.md"] becomes moot once AGENTS.md exists'
The `project_doc_fallback_filenames` key does not appear anywhere in the repo
findings.finding: `.codex/config.toml` does not exist as a repo-tracked file. The plan names it as a primary edit target and describes its content (`project_doc_fallback_filenames=["CLAUDE.md"]`, 'temporary/until migrated' comment on lines 3-6), but no such file exists in the repo (the only Codex-related file is the user-install template at `scripts/session-shims/codex-config.toml`, which contains only `[shell_environment_policy]`). The plan may be conflating the user-level `~/.codex/config.toml` with a repo-tracked file.
findings.location: What section item (1); De-risking results section: 'Codex: .codex/config.toml's project_doc_fallback_filenames=["CLAUDE.md"]'; AC: '.codex/config.toml reads AGENTS.md natively'
scenarios: An implementer following the plan searches for `.codex/config.toml` and finds nothing, or edits the user-level `~/.codex/config.toml` which is not a repo artifact and cannot satisfy the AC prove command.
CI command `rg -c 'CLAUDE.md|temporary' .codex/config.toml` in AC1 will exit non-zero (file not found) — making the AC unprovable.
findings.suggested_fix: If the intent is to add a repo-tracked Codex project config, create `.codex/config.toml` as a new file. If the intent is to update the user-install template, retarget the edit to `scripts/session-shims/codex-config.toml` and update the AC prove command to match.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm whether `.agents/rules/rebar.md` pre-exists (add it to a predecessor story or note it must be created by this story) and update the plan accordingly — if creation is intended, call it out explicitly in the Scope as new behavior.
evidence: Zero search hits for `.agents/rules/rebar.md` or the `.agents/` directory anywhere in the repo
Plan quote: 'Reconcile .agents/rules/rebar.md with the new AGENTS.md (reduce to a pointer or align content; eliminate drift)'
Predecessor stories named: 'the AGENTS.md story and the CLAUDE.local.md story' — neither produces `.agents/rules/rebar.md`
The Why section says 'hand-maintained neutral distillation that must not drift' — implying it currently exists
findings.finding: `.agents/rules/rebar.md` does not exist in the repo and is not a deliverable of the predecessor stories (which produce `AGENTS.md` and update `CLAUDE.local.md`). The plan names it as an edit target to 'reconcile' with `AGENTS.md`, implying it must pre-exist.
findings.location: What section item (2); Scope 'In:' list; original AC bullet '.agents/rules/rebar.md is consistent with AGENTS.md'
scenarios: An implementer cannot reconcile a file that doesn't exist; the AC covering `.agents/rules/rebar.md` consistency cannot be verified.
If the file is meant to be created by this story (new behavior), that makes it CREATION=out-of-scope per the plan's own framing as a 'reconcile' task.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `docs/plan-review-criteria-guide.md:467` to the explicit criteria-text update list alongside `criteria_routing.json:407` and `plan_review_T5e.md:10`.
evidence: `docs/plan-review-criteria-guide.md:467`: '- A novel architectural decision is captured in an ADR / CLAUDE.md / design-doc update.'
The plan lists `criteria_routing.json:407` and `plan_review_T5e.md:10` but not `docs/plan-review-criteria-guide.md`
The guide is named in the ~15 files list only as a general CLAUDE.md-reference file, not as a criteria-text file to update
findings.finding: The plan's `~15 files` reference-surface enumeration in the de-risking section omits `docs/plan-review-criteria-guide.md:467`, which contains a live `CLAUDE.md` reference ('A novel architectural decision is captured in an ADR / CLAUDE.md / design-doc update.'). The plan separately names `criteria_routing.json:407` and `plan_review_T5e.md:10` for the same text, but `plan-review-criteria-guide.md` is the human-readable version of that criterion and also needs updating.
findings.location: De-risking results: '~15 files' enumeration; also 'Also update the plan-review CRITERIA text' paragraph
scenarios: Implementer updates `criteria_routing.json` and `plan_review_T5e.md` but leaves `docs/plan-review-criteria-guide.md:467` pointing at CLAUDE.md, creating inconsistency in the human-readable guide.
findings.suggested_fix: In the 'Also update the plan-review CRITERIA text' paragraph, add: '`docs/plan-review-criteria-guide.md:467` (the human-readable mirror of the same check text) → change "CLAUDE.md" to "AGENTS.md"'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a glob exclusion for the experiments corpus to the AC's grep command (e.g. `--glob '!docs/experiments/'`) or explicitly note in the AC that experiment-corpus hits are intentionally excluded as historical/versioned data and need not be updated.
evidence: De-risking: 'excludes the `.rebar/cache/`, `bridge_state/snapshots/`, and `docs/experiments/` corpus, which are data'
AC: '`rg -n 'CLAUDE\.md' --glob '!CLAUDE.md'` reviewed line-by-line in the change'
docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/criteria/criteria_v7.json:465: "check": "A novel architectural decision is captured in an ADR / CLAUDE.md / design-doc update."
docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/criteria/criteria_v8.json:500: same pattern
docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/criteria/make_crit_v7.py:181: same pattern
findings.finding: The AC's proving command (`rg -n 'CLAUDE\.md' --glob '!CLAUDE.md'`) will surface hits in `docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/criteria/` (criteria_v7.json:465, criteria_v8.json:500, make_crit_v7.py:181), but the de-risking narrative pre-excludes the experiments corpus as 'data'. An implementer doing the mandated line-by-line review has no instruction on whether to update these hits or mark them intentional, creating an ambiguous implementation path.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria (revised): `rg -n 'CLAUDE\.md' --glob '!CLAUDE.md'` reviewed line-by-line; De-risking results: 'excludes the `docs/experiments/` corpus, which are data'
scenarios: Implementer runs the AC grep, sees experiment-corpus hits, and either updates historical/versioned JSON data (wrong) or silently skips them without recording the decision (violates the 'every reference intentional or updated' AC).
A future AC audit re-runs the grep and treats unupdated experiment hits as an open finding.
findings.suggested_fix: Amend the AC rg command to: `rg -n 'CLAUDE\.md' --glob '!CLAUDE.md' --glob '!docs/experiments/'` and add a parenthetical: '(experiment-corpus JSON/scripts are historical data; hits there are intentionally excluded)'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a one-sentence positive rationale for the pointer-not-duplicate requirement for optional per-tool files (e.g., 'to prevent content drift and maintain a single source of truth in AGENTS.md').
evidence: ABSENCE: No sentence in the plan explains why pointers are preferred over duplicated content for optional per-tool files (e.g., to avoid drift, reduce maintenance burden, single source of truth).
Plan states the constraint but not the reasoning: 'any added must be pointers to AGENTS.md, not duplicated content. Keep the module-size gate and all CI green.'
findings.finding: The plan asserts 'any added [per-tool pointer files] must be pointers to AGENTS.md, not duplicated content' but provides no positive rationale for this design choice. The rubric requires the plan to state why the chosen approach fits, not merely what it is.
findings.location: What section, item (4): 'any added must be pointers to AGENTS.md, not duplicated content'; Acceptance Criteria item 4: 'any added are pointers, not duplicated content'
scenarios: An implementer or reviewer questions why a tool like Copilot couldn't have its own richer file and finds no documented rationale, leading to re-litigation of the design decision during review.
findings.suggested_fix: In the What section item (4), append: 'Pointers are used rather than duplicated content to prevent divergence and keep AGENTS.md as the single source of truth.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify and cite the official Gemini IDE / Gemini Code Assist documentation for `context.fileName` (or equivalent) before committing to this pointer mechanism; if no such convention exists, remove the Gemini pointer from the decision surface.
evidence: Plan text: 'Decide and record whether to add optional per-tool pointers: .github/copilot-instructions.md (for Copilot IN-EDITOR) and a Gemini `context.fileName` note'
Zero repo hits for 'context.fileName' across entire codebase
No citation to official Gemini IDE or Gemini Code Assist documentation for this convention
Prior cached finding (.rebar/cache/plan-review/5b01-add0-e970-44bd/4d3e53998e702be9205f.json) independently surfaced this same gap
findings.finding: The plan asserts a Gemini 'context.fileName note' as an optional per-tool pointer mechanism, but this is an unverified capability assertion: no such Gemini IDE convention is documented in the repo, no link to official Gemini docs is provided, and the repo contains zero hits for 'context.fileName'.
scenarios: An implementer searches for the Gemini 'context.fileName' convention and finds it doesn't exist as described, wasting time or creating a file that Gemini tooling ignores entirely.
The AC ('Decision on optional Copilot/Cursor/Gemini per-tool files is recorded') is satisfied by recording a decision to add a Gemini pointer file that has no actual effect.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace the vague 'a Gemini `context.fileName` note' with either a cited official Gemini Code Assist convention (e.g., the documented `CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md` or context-file feature, if one exists) or drop it and record in the decision log that no verified Gemini in-editor pointer mechanism was found.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] For the Gemini `context.fileName` pointer: either name the specific Gemini product (e.g., Gemini Code Assist for VS Code, `gemini` CLI) and add a vendor docs URL + a proving step to the AC, or explicitly record in the AC that the Gemini pointer is deferred/out-of-scope pending vendor verification.
evidence: Plan text: 'a Gemini `context.fileName` note' — no product name, no vendor doc citation, no file path specified
Search for `context.fileName` returns zero hits across the entire repo
No `.gemini/` directory or Gemini config file exists anywhere in the repo
Contrast: `.github/copilot-instructions.md` is cited with its known reading mechanism (Copilot IN-EDITOR); the Gemini analogue is never grounded at the same level
Evidence class: Unverified
findings.finding: The plan proposes adding 'a Gemini `context.fileName` note' as an optional per-tool pointer to AGENTS.md, but names no specific Gemini product that reads this field, provides no vendor documentation URL, and zero repo hits exist for `context.fileName`. This is an unverified external-capability assertion: an implementer cannot determine which Gemini tool (Gemini Code Assist, Gemini CLI, Google AI Studio, etc.) would consume the file or how to verify it is active after the change.
findings.location: What section, item (4): 'a Gemini `context.fileName` note'; AC item: 'Decision on optional Copilot/Cursor/Gemini per-tool files is recorded'
scenarios: An implementer adds a `context.json` or `GEMINI.md` file (guessing the format) pointing to AGENTS.md, but the target Gemini product never reads it — dead config surface is created
A reviewer cannot verify the 'any added are pointers, not duplicated content' AC because the file path and reading tool are unknown
If the Gemini product requires a specific JSON schema with a `context.fileName` key, a plain Markdown pointer file would be silently ignored
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the AC for the Gemini pointer: 'If added, cite the specific Gemini product + vendor docs URL confirming it reads a `context.fileName` field, and add a proving command (e.g., `cat .gemini/context.json | jq .fileName`). Otherwise record the decision as deferred.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the canonical replacement text for the decision-rule string in `criteria_routing.json:407`, `plan_review_T5e.md:10`, and `docs/plan-review-criteria-guide.md:467` (e.g. `"…captured in an ADR / AGENTS.md / design-doc update"`), so all three copies receive an identical, verifiable update.
evidence: Plan text: 'criteria_routing.json:407 and src/rebar/llm/reviewers/plan_review_T5e.md:10 cite "an ADR / CLAUDE.md / design-doc" → change to reference AGENTS.md'
Confirmed by read of criteria_routing.json:407: check value is 'A novel architectural decision is captured in an ADR / CLAUDE.md / design-doc update.'
Confirmed by read of plan_review_T5e.md:10: inline prompt contains 'a novel architectural decision is captured in an ADR / CLAUDE.md / design doc update'
No replacement string is anywhere stated in the plan — the plan describes the action ('change to reference AGENTS.md') but not the exact new text.
findings.finding: The plan names `criteria_routing.json:407` and `plan_review_T5e.md:10` as update targets but never specifies the replacement text for the decision-rule string `"A novel architectural decision is captured in an ADR / CLAUDE.md / design-doc update."` These strings are live machine-readable check values consumed by the LLM plan-review pipeline; an executor must guess the canonical AGENTS.md-aware wording.
findings.location: De-risking results section: 'Also update the plan-review CRITERIA text … criteria_routing.json:407 and plan_review_T5e.md:10 cite "an ADR / CLAUDE.md / design-doc" → change to reference AGENTS.md'
scenarios: An agent implementing this plan replaces 'CLAUDE.md' with 'AGENTS.md' but leaves 'ADR / AGENTS.md / design-doc' — a partial fix that may not match what other prompt versions say (docs/plan-review-criteria-guide.md:467 is a third copy).
The three copies of this string (criteria_routing.json, plan_review_T5e.md, docs/plan-review-criteria-guide.md) may receive inconsistent rewrites because the canonical replacement is never stated.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the plan: 'Replace the check string with: "A novel architectural decision is captured in an ADR / AGENTS.md / design-doc update." Apply identically to criteria_routing.json:407, plan_review_T5e.md:10, and docs/plan-review-criteria-guide.md:467.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Either cite the Gemini IDE documentation confirming `context.fileName` is a real configuration convention (with the expected file path/format), or remove it from the plan's optional deliverable list and note it as unresearched.
evidence: Plan text: 'Decide and record whether to add optional per-tool pointers: … a Gemini `context.fileName` note'
Zero codebase hits for 'context.fileName' outside of .rebar/cache commentary files that themselves flag it as an unverified assertion.
The .rebar/cache hits show prior review passes already identified this as an unknown capability: 'no such Gemini IDE convention is documented'
findings.finding: The plan references a 'Gemini `context.fileName` note' as a concrete optional per-tool pointer deliverable, but this Gemini configuration convention has no definition anywhere in the codebase, no referenced specification, and no value vocabulary — it is an undefined mechanism named as a plan action.
findings.location: What section, item (4): 'a Gemini `context.fileName` note; any added must be pointers to `AGENTS.md`, not duplicated content.'
scenarios: An implementer attempts to add a Gemini context.fileName note but finds no such Gemini configuration mechanism exists, causing wasted investigation or a silently wrong file.
The AC says 'decision on optional Copilot/Cursor/Gemini per-tool files is recorded' but with no definition of what a valid Gemini pointer file looks like, the acceptance criterion is unverifiable.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
